title: Data & statistics
subtitle: Read tables, find averages, notice odd values and explain what the numbers mean.
curriculumLinks: MCS.5: select and draw appropriate displays
MCS.5: calculate mean, median, mode and range
MCS.5: discuss misconceptions and misuse of statistics

diagrams.title: Read a bar chart
diagrams.description: Compare heights carefully and always check the scale on the axis.

diagrams.title: Find the median
diagrams.description: Put the values in order, then find the middle value.

diagrams.title: Spot an outlier
diagrams.description: An outlier sits far away from the main group and needs explaining.

rules.title: Mean is the balancing average
rules.explanation: Add all the values and divide by how many values there are. The mean can be pulled up or down by one very unusual value.
steps: Count how many values there are.
Divide the total by the count.

rules.title: Median is the middle value
rules.explanation: Put the values in order. The median is the middle value. If there are two middle values, add them and divide by 2.
steps: Write the values from smallest to biggest.
Cross off one at each end until the middle is left.
For two middle values, find their mean.

rules.title: Range shows the spread
rules.explanation: The range tells you how far apart the biggest and smallest values are.
steps: Subtract the lowest from the highest.

rules.title: An outlier is unusual
rules.explanation: An outlier is a value that does not fit the pattern. It may be a real result or it may be a recording mistake.
rules.formula: look for the value far away from the rest
steps: Look at the values in order.
Find any value far away from the others.
Explain why it might be unusual in the context.

examples.question: Temperatures are 6, 7, 6, 50, 8, 7.
examples.working: 50 is far away from the other temperatures.
examples.answer: 50 is likely to be a mistake or a different unit.
